title: vernonia cinerea subsp. vialis
intro: Vernonia cinerea subsp. vialis, also known as the field Vernonia or Varnia, is a plant species that belongs to the daisy family and is characterized by its yellow flowers and hairy leaves. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for centuries, particularly in India and other parts of Asia, due to its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antibacterial effects. The health benefits of Vernonia cinerea subsp. vialis include reducing inflammation, treating skin conditions such as eczema and acne, and even exhibiting potential anti-cancer properties. Vernonia cinerea subsp. vialis is often prepared as a herbal tea or infusion, and its leaves and flowers can also be used to make a decoction, which is said to have various therapeutic benefits.
